title: eremophila alatisepala
intro: Eremophila alatisepala, also known as the Winged Emu Bush, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in Western Australian herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health, due to its high concentration of iridoid glycosides and terpenoids. Traditionally, the plant was used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to make use of its medicinal properties.
